name: Salicylate sensor (NahR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects salicylate (a naphthalene-degradation marker and drug metabolite). The NahR LysR-family activator gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a visible pigment, in safe E. coli K-12.

input.detects: Salicylate in water (naphthalene/PAH degradation intermediate; also a drug metabolite)

sensing.sensorMechanism: NahR (LysR family) binds salicylate and activates the Psal/Pnah promoters.
sensing.signalFlow: Salicylate -> NahR activates Psal -> transcribes an anti-pigment sgRNA -> CRISPRi represses a constitutive amilCP cassette -> pigment fades with salicylate (NOT). Pair an inverter for colour-on.

performance.notes: NahR is a classic, well-characterised salicylate sensor; integrated CRISPRi device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: Lab/contained; add kill-switch for field use.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-pigment sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the amilCP promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: NahR + Psal -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9 + constitutive amilCP.

buildSteps.title: Transform E. coli K-12
buildSteps.description: Select; confirm baseline pigment without salicylate.

provenance.source: Design template combining the NahR salicylate-sensing module with E. coli CRISPRi
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
